        Project Manager

        Mott MacDonald
        Leeds, West Yorkshire
        Competitive
        As a Project Manager you will manage your own projects or workstreams on major infrastructure projects. Our North Eastern transportation team is expanding, and we have multiple exciting opportunities for project and commercial managers. Our core team consists of professionally qualified and experienced Project/Programme Managers, Planners, Commercial Managers, and Quantity Surveyors. The focus of this exciting new role and opportunity is to help build the growing business and to support our Clients by winning and delivering high quality project and programme management services that contribute to society and make the communities in which we live and work better. We lead and deliver a broad range of exciting transportation projects and programmes through our integrated, full service, Project, Programme, and Commercial Management (PPCM).

        Description The Project Manager is responsible for planning, executing, and delivering assigned engineering projects within agreed scope, budget, and timelines. The Project Manager will coordinate closely with internal teams, clients, subcontractors, and suppliers. This role involves managing resources, risks, budgets, and stakeholder relationships to ensure projects meet quality, safety, and commercial objectives. Technical Responsibilities - Project Planning & Execution * Develop project plans including scope, objectives, schedules, milestones, and resource allocation. * Ensure projects comply with technical, safety, and quality requirements. * Monitor progress and make adjustments to meet project objectives. * Prepare and manage project budgets, cost forecasts, and cash flow. * Monitor project costs and report variances to senior management. * Identify project risks and develop mitigation plans.

This is an office-based role, with no previous hands-on technical experience required, and a background within managing projects within the construction/engineering sector would be ideal. * Coordination of major electrical infrastructure projects from conception to client handover * Completing and issuing documentation including RAMS, drawings, Street Works licences and project progress reports * Procurement of necessary project materials and labour, including subcontractors * Assigning schedule of works and monitoring project completion timeframes

        1. Jobs

        3. Project Manager

        What is the average salary for Project Manager in UK?

        Average salary per year
        £54,657
        The average salary for a Project Manager in UK is £54,657. Project Manager in UK salaries range from £42,499 to £70,310.

        Find out more

        How many Project Manager jobs are there in UK?
        There are 6,755 Project Manager jobs within 10 miles of UK available on Totaljobs right now.
        Where in UK are these jobs?
        You can find jobs in South East, Midlands, North West.
        Which industry do Project Manager jobs in UK belong to?
        Project Manager jobs in UK are part of the Management industry.
        How many temporary and part-time Project Manager jobs are available in UK?
        There are 65 temporary Project Manager jobs and 1043 part-time Project Manager jobs available in UK at the moment.
        Essential skills for a project manager career
        Successful project managers possess a blend of technical and soft skills. Key competencies include communication, organisation, leadership, and problem-solving abilities, alongside proficiency in project management methodologies and software tools.
        What does a typical day look like for a project manager?
        A project manager's day involves coordinating teams, monitoring progress, and addressing issues. They attend meetings, update stakeholders, manage budgets, and ensure projects stay on track, balancing multiple responsibilities throughout their workday.
        How to start a career in project management
        Begin by gaining relevant qualifications and experience in your chosen industry. Pursue certifications like PMP or PRINCE2, seek entry-level positions such as assistant project manager or project coordinator, and continuously develop your skills.
        Different roles in the project management industry
        The field offers diverse opportunities, from construction project manager to project programme manager. Roles such as projects manager, manager project management, and project management officer cater to various sectors and specialisations within the industry.

        Get hired.
